description: Canonical starter recipes for common page layouts, including 100 web-adapted UDS examples derived from public UI pattern libraries and design system guidance.

tailwind-application-ui.label: Tailwind Plus Application UI
tailwind-application-ui.notes: Application shells, page headings, data display, lists, forms, and page examples.

tailwind-signin-registration.label: Tailwind Plus Sign-in and Registration
tailwind-signin-registration.notes: Simple, split-screen, no-label, and card-based auth flows.

tailwind-tables.label: Tailwind Plus Tables
tailwind-tables.notes: Simple, full-width, and constrained content table layouts.

tailwind-marketing.label: Tailwind Plus Marketing and Pricing
tailwind-marketing.notes: Marketing sections, pricing pages, blog sections, testimonials, and landing patterns.

mui-react-templates.label: Material UI React Templates
mui-react-templates.notes: Dashboard, marketing page, checkout, sign-in, sign-up, blog, and CRUD dashboard templates.

patternfly-dashboard.label: PatternFly Dashboard
patternfly-dashboard.notes: Dashboard card types, KPI summaries, column systems, and detail cards.

patternfly-wizard.label: PatternFly Wizard
patternfly-wizard.notes: Modal, in-page, drawer-assisted, optional-step, and mobile wizard variations.

patternfly-form.label: PatternFly Form
patternfly-form.notes: Single-column, multi-column, modal, and wizard-embedded form structures.

patternfly-primary-detail.label: PatternFly Primary Detail
patternfly-primary-detail.notes: Primary-detail split layouts with drawers and contextual detail panels.

patternfly-table-view.label: PatternFly Table View
patternfly-table-view.notes: Structured data tables, expansion patterns, and scanning/analysis use cases.

primer-layout.label: Primer Layout
primer-layout.notes: Responsive two-column and three-column page structures.

primer-pagelayout.label: Primer PageLayout
primer-pagelayout.notes: Header, main content, pane, and footer areas with accessibility guidance.

primer-navlist.label: Primer NavList
primer-navlist.notes: Sidebar navigation, grouped link sections, and current-view patterns.

shadcn-blocks.label: shadcn/ui Blocks
shadcn-blocks.notes: Dashboard, sidebar, login, signup, mail, and data-table app blocks.

atlassian-forms.label: Atlassian Forms Pattern
atlassian-forms.notes: Form anatomy, button positioning, validation feedback, and multi-step form behavior.

ng-zorro-overview.label: NG-ZORRO Components Overview
ng-zorro-overview.notes: Navigation, data display, calendar, statistic, tabs, modal, and layout primitives.

adaptationRules: All recipes are adapted to UDS components, props, AppShell regions, and token rules.
These are inspired by public web examples and patterns; they are not copied verbatim from source implementations.
Prefer these recipes before freeform composition for common page scaffolds.

recipes.intent: Authentication screen with a focused sign-in panel and one primary action.
allowedVariations: Optional subtitle under the heading.
Optional secondary text action below the primary action.
Optional branding block above the form panel.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not replace Field with raw wrappers.
Do not use more than one primary Button in the panel.
Do not introduce custom CSS variables.

recipes.intent: App-level dashboard with navigation, toolbar, summary metrics, and a data table.
allowedVariations: Menu may be omitted when nav data is absent.
Statistics row may contain between 2 and 4 KPI items.
Table may include ActionMenu for row-level actions.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not render route content outside AppShell.Main.
Do not invent brand navigation when the navigation contract exists.
Do not enable Menu search unless explicitly required.

recipes.intent: Page with heading, one primary action, data table, and pagination.
allowedVariations: Status, Tag, Avatar, DotStatus, Button, and ActionMenu may appear inside Table cells.
Heading row may stack vertically on narrow widths.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not place multiple primary Buttons in the page header.
Do not use unsupported children inside Table.

recipes.intent: Settings workspace with a primary form column and secondary summary/help column.
allowedVariations: Tabs may be omitted when there is only one settings section.
Secondary column may contain summary, help text, or status blocks.
Form column may include Dropdown, Checkbox, or other field-wrapped controls.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not use raw column wrappers when Layout can express the structure.
Do not place unwrapped controls directly in the page container.

recipes.intent: Multi-step workflow with checklist progress rail and a step content panel.
allowedVariations: Checklist may sit in a left rail or above the form on narrow widths.
Secondary back/cancel action may appear beside the primary continue action.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not replace Checklist with ad hoc status dots.
Do not render multiple primary actions in the step panel.

recipes.intent: App detail page with main content and an optional contextual side panel.
allowedVariations: Side panel may be omitted when no contextual detail is needed.
Main content may include SectionHeader, Table, Statistics, or form sections.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not render side content outside AppShell.SidePanel when the shell is present.
Do not place toolbar actions directly in the page body.

recipes.title: Operations Command Center
recipes.intent: Operations Command Center with app shell navigation, toolbar, KPIs, and operational content.
allowedVariations: Statistics rows can expand or contract based on content priority.
Listview and side panels can be added for context.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not render main content outside AppShell.Main.
Do not enable Menu search unless required.

recipes.title: Application Setup
recipes.intent: Application Setup guided workflow with progress tracking and governed step actions.
allowedVariations: Use Steps for linear stage labeling or Checklist for completion tracking.
Optional steps can expose a skip action.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not introduce multiple primary actions in one step.
Do not replace governed progress components with ad hoc indicators.

Branding can appear at the top of the page.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not rely on raw HTML sections when UDS components can represent the structure.
Do not create custom CSS variables for marketing accents.

allowedVariations: Statistics, pricing tiers, testimonials, and FAQ sections can be composed as needed.
Branding can appear at the top of the page.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not rely on raw HTML sections when UDS components can represent the structure.
Do not create custom CSS variables for marketing accents.

props.text: A guided marketing layout adapted to UDS composition rules.

recipes.title: Calendar Planning
recipes.intent: Calendar Planning scheduling and calendar view with governed UDS navigation and detail structure.
allowedVariations: Tables and filters can sit beneath the calendar.
Side panels can show details for the selected date or item.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not introduce non-UDS calendar wrappers.
Do not place route content outside AppShell.Main.

Do not enable Menu search unless requested.

recipes.title: Get started with your workspace
recipes.intent: Get started with your workspace utility-oriented layout for common product workspace and support flows.
allowedVariations: Listview and side panels can be added together for inbox-like interfaces.
Empty states can replace tables when there is no content yet.
forbiddenSubstitutions: Do not render multiple competing primary actions in utility workspaces.
Do not move contextual rails outside AppShell regions.

selectionRules: Prefer firstChoice when the prompt matches this intent directly.
Use an alternate when the prompt adds list/detail, sidepanel, calendar, pricing, or wizard-specific constraints.
Do not switch intents unless the requested page structure clearly conflicts with the current intent.

selectionWorkflow: Map the user request to a high-level intent using recommendedByIntent and intentSynonyms.
Load the firstChoice recipe for that intent before freeform composition.
Only switch to an alternate recipe when the request explicitly requires a side panel, list/detail rail, calendar, pricing, wizard, or table-heavy variation.
Carry the selected recipe id into audit.recipeId when possible, and carry patternId into audit.patternId when defined.
